Home / Services / Drainage Solutions

Drainage Solutions

Solving drainage problems to protect your property from water damage.

Excess water can cause significant damage to your property, leading to foundation problems, soil erosion, and landscaping issues. Wills Excavating provides effective drainage solutions to protect your Indianapolis property from water damage. We offer a range of drainage services, including French drains, surface drains, and grading adjustments. Our team assesses your property's drainage issues and designs a customized solution to effectively manage water runoff.

We use high-quality materials and proven techniques to ensure long-lasting results. Wills Excavating understands the importance of proper drainage in Indianapolis, with its heavy rainfall and clay soil. We take into account the local climate and soil conditions when designing and installing drainage systems. Whether you're experiencing standing water in your yard, water infiltration into your basement, or erosion problems, we can provide a solution to keep your property dry and protected.

Our Process

  1. 01 Step 1: Site Assessment & Diagnosis. Identifying the source of the drainage problem.
  2. 02 Step 2: System Design. Designing a customized drainage solution.
  3. 03 Step 3: Installation. Installing the drainage system using high-quality materials.
  4. 04 Step 4: Testing & Adjustment. Testing the system to ensure it is functioning properly.

Benefits

  • Prevents water damage to your property
  • Reduces soil erosion
  • Eliminates standing water
  • Improves landscaping

Frequently Asked Questions

What is a French drain?

A French drain is a trench filled with gravel or rock that contains a perforated pipe. It is used to redirect surface and groundwater away from a specific area.

How do I know if I need a drainage system?

Signs that you may need a drainage system include standing water in your yard, water infiltration into your basement, soil erosion, and foundation cracks.

How much does it cost to install a drainage system?

The cost depends on the complexity of the project and the type of drainage system installed. We provide free estimates to help you understand the cost involved.

Get a Free Estimate
